perturbation.original_text: Promoter is an independent contractor and not an officer, director, affiliate, insider, employee or agent of VNUE or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates. Promoter has no authority to, and will not, enter into contracts, make representations, warranties or commitments purporting to be binding on VNUE or otherwise act on VNUE's behalf and shall not take any action that might lead third parties to believe Promoter has the right to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: Promoter is an independent contractor and not an officer, director, affiliate, insider, employee or agent of VNUE or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates. Promoter has limited authority to enter into contracts, make representations, warranties or commitments purporting to be binding on VNUE with prior written approval from VNUE's CEO, otherwise act on VNUE's behalf and shall not take any action that might lead third parties to believe Promoter has the right to do so.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the Promoter's authority. Initially, the Promoter had no authority to enter contracts. Now, the Promoter has limited authority with CEO approval, contradicting the original statement and creating ambiguity about the extent of their permissible actions. This creates a contradiction later in the agreement where Section 1 states that VNUE engages the promoter as an 'Independent Contractor to secure contracts' implying a level of authority now contradicted by the limited authority clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Promotion Services. Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement: A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Independent Contractor to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues. B. The Promoter will provide certain skills, expertise, experience and abilities developed as global leader in the music and entertainment business over two decades. C. The Promoter will consult and work with the Directors and Officers of VNUE concerning matters relating to business development and other matters deemed necessary to perform the Promotion Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Promoter has no authority to enter into contracts, while the changed text grants the Promoter limited authority with CEO approval. This directly contradicts Section 1, which states that VNUE engages the Promoter 'to secure contracts', implying a broader authority. The introduction of 'limited authority' creates a significant inconsistency and potential legal issue regarding the scope of the Promoter's power to bind VNUE to contracts, fitting the definition of ambiguities.

perturbation.original_text: A. Promoter will disclose to VNUE any and all material facts and circumstances, which may affect its ability to perform its undertaking herein.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Promoter will disclose to VNUE any and all known material facts and circumstances, which may affect its ability to perform its undertaking herein. However, the Promoter is not obligated to actively seek out such facts and circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a caveat to the Promoter's disclosure obligations. The original text requires disclosure of 'any and all' material facts, whereas the modified text limits this to 'known' facts and removes the obligation to actively seek them out. This contradicts the original intent of ensuring full transparency, as the promoter is not liable for unknown facts, even if they affect their performance and now contradicts the entire section 8, since it questions the "Competent Work" nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8. Competent Work. All work will be done in a competent fashion in accordance with applicable standards of the profession. Promoter represents, warrants, and covenants the following: A. Promoter will disclose to VNUE any and all known material facts and circumstances, which may affect its ability to perform its undertaking herein. However, the Promoter is not obligated to actively seek out such facts and circumstances. B. Promoter is an independent contractor acting in the limited capacity as an independent contractor for VNUE. C. Promoter will not disseminate or share with third parties any material information about VNUE not already contained in a Company report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, as Promoter acknowledges that such third parties might try to act on such material non-public information by engaging in "insider trading" to the detriment of VNUE.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8A required the Promoter to disclose 'any and all' material facts, creating a clear and comprehensive obligation. The modified text limits this disclosure to 'known' facts and removes the obligation to actively seek them out. This creates an in-text contradiction because the revised text potentially undermines the 'Competent Work' standard in Section 8 by allowing the Promoter to remain unaware of and thus fail to disclose material facts that could impact their performance. The revised text introduces ambiguity and allows the Promoter to be less accountable, which contradicts the initial intention of ensuring competent work through full transparency, thus creating a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for reasonable expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an accounting of expenses incurred, at a frequency determined by VNUE's accounting department, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within a reasonable timeframe, as determined by VNUE's accounting department, of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines clear and specific terms for expense reimbursement, including weekly itemized accounting and 30-day reimbursement. The modified text introduces ambiguity by using terms like 'reasonable expenses' and 'reasonable timeframe,' and giving VNUE's accounting department authority to define the frequency of accounting and reimbursement timeframe. This directly contradicts the original terms and makes it unclear when and how the Promoter will be reimbursed. This also affects Section 1, as it brings contradiction on the consideration, since, the consideration is not concrete
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Promotion Services. Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement: A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Independent Contractor to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues. B. The Promoter will provide certain skills, expertise, experience and abilities developed as global leader in the music and entertainment business over two decades. C. The Promoter will consult and work with the Directors and Officers of VNUE concerning matters relating to business development and other matters deemed necessary to perform the Promotion Services.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces significant ambiguity where the original text had clear and specific terms. By changing 'weekly itemized accounting' to an accounting frequency 'determined by VNUE's accounting department' and 'within Thirty (30) Days' to a 'reasonable timeframe, as determined by VNUE's accounting department,' the modified text contradicts the expectation of certainty established in Section 1, where the Promoter is engaged to provide services 'in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement'. This falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as it makes enforcement difficult and interpretation unclear.
